name: Maximum Depth in BT
category: Binary Trees [Traversals, Medium and Hard Problems]
statement:
Given root of the binary tree, return its maximum depth.


A binary tree's maximum depth is number of nodes along the longest path from root node down to the farthest node.

examples.explanation: The path from root node 1 to node with value 6 has maximum depth with 3 nodes along path.
The path from root node 3 to node with value 15 has maximum depth with 3 nodes along path. There exists other paths to reach the solution.

nowYourTurn.input: root = [5, 1, 2, 8, null, null, 5, null, 4, null, null, 7]
